description: Wave 7 — Public Live League (v2 MVP). Turns the deterministic benchmark engine into a public spectating product: paced replay of committed events as 'live' broadcast, a curated-roster compounding game bank driving an ELO-style OpenSkill ladder, and deterministic Town-of-Salem analytics with sampled LLM-judge enrichment. Scope per prd-v2.md and locked decisions (loop builds UI Playwright-gated; invite/curated roster; anonymous MVP; house-funded $200 hard cap). DEFERRED to a later wave: open submissions, OAuth identity, anti-Sybil, accounts/profiles. Build order: Phase 0 contract -> Phase 1 broadcast spine -> Phase 2 safety+cost rails (gate public launch) -> Phase 3 matchmaking+ladder -> Phase 4 analytics -> Phase 5 hardening. See prd-v2.md sections 2 (decisions), 3 (phases), 6 (human-dependency register).

userStories.title: Versioned public_event_v1 projection contract
userStories.description: As a platform building live views, replays, analytics and ratings on top of the event log, I need a STABLE, versioned public projection of game events distinct from internal core events, so internal schema changes never become public-API migrations.
acceptanceCriteria: New module `src/padrino/public/projection.py` (impure-adjacent, pure-ish) exposing `to_public_event_v1(event: Mapping) -> dict | None` built on top of `core.spectator_projection.project_event_for_spectator` (identity-blind: never leaks role/faction/model/provider/build-id of others).

Each emitted dict carries `schema_version: "public_event_v1"`, `sequence: int`, `phase`, `event_type`, `visibility`, `actor_player_id|null`, `payload`, and the hash-chain fields (`prev_event_hash`, `event_hash`).
A `PUBLIC_EVENT_FORBIDDEN_KEYS` allow/deny set is asserted; mid-game role/faction reveal is impossible (regression-guards the prior P0 spectator leak).
Golden-file test `tests/public/test_projection_contract.py` pins the exact public shape for a recorded game; any field change must update the golden file consciously.
Hypothesis property test: for every internal event, the public projection contains no key in the forbidden set.
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run ruff format --check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes
userStories.notes: D2 / Phase 0. The stable seam every later phase reads from.

userStories.title: Spoiler-safe public game broadcast index
userStories.description: As a spectator watching a game 'live' (paced replay), I must NOT be able to learn the outcome early via any other endpoint, so live tension is preserved even though the result is already committed in the DB.
acceptanceCriteria: New `BroadcastState` enum/column on a public index (LIVE, RECENT, HIDDEN) derived from a game's broadcast lifecycle, not its true `status`.

While a game is LIVE, public endpoints MUST NOT expose `terminal_result`, winner, final ratings delta, or any not-yet-streamed event (enforced by sequence high-water-mark per viewer is out of scope here; index-level outcome hiding is in scope).
Repository `src/padrino/public/broadcast_index.py` with `list_live()`, `list_recent(limit)`, and `mark_*` transitions; pure query layer, no scheduler coupling.
Test `tests/public/test_broadcast_index_spoiler.py` asserts a LIVE game's public payload omits outcome fields and that a RECENT game exposes them.
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run ruff format --check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes
userStories.notes: Spoiler policy (council). Outcome hidden until broadcast completes.

userStories.title: Broadcaster core: deterministic paced stream generator
userStories.description: As the broadcast layer, I need a PURE, deterministic generator that turns a game's committed events into a paced sequence of public_event_v1 frames with inter-frame delays, so pacing logic is unit-testable with zero I/O and the runner stays untouched.
acceptanceCriteria: New `src/padrino/public/broadcaster.py::plan_broadcast(events, cadence) -> list[BroadcastFrame]` where each frame has `(public_event_v1, delay_ms)`; cadence config carries per-event-type delays (e.g. chat line vs phase change).
Pure function: no clock reads, no sleeps, no DB — delays are DATA, applied by the transport layer (US-089).
Cadence defaults live in settings (`padrino_broadcast_cadence_*`), human-tunable; sane defaults shipped (e.g. ~2.5s per public message).
Test `tests/public/test_broadcaster_plan.py` asserts frame count == projected-event count, ordering preserved, and delays match config for each event type.
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run ruff format --check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes
userStories.notes: D1 spine. Production/presentation decoupled; engine not modified.

userStories.title: SSE live endpoint, resumable by sequence cursor
userStories.description: As a spectator (incl. one who reconnects), I need a Server-Sent-Events endpoint that streams a game's broadcast frames at the planned cadence and lets me resume from where I left off, so reconnection doesn't replay from zero.
acceptanceCriteria: New route `GET /public/games/{game_id}/live` (in `src/padrino/api/routes/public.py` or a new `public_live.py`) returning `text/event-stream`; each SSE message is one public_event_v1 frame with its `sequence` as the SSE `id:`.
Honors `Last-Event-ID` / `?after=<sequence>` to resume; never replays already-sent frames.
Refuses (404/409) games not in LIVE/RECENT broadcast state; identity-blind via US-086 only.
Transport applies the per-frame delay from US-088; a `?speed=` debug multiplier is allowed but capped.
Test `tests/api/test_public_live_sse.py` drives the endpoint with a stubbed clock, asserts frame order, resume-after-cursor correctness, and that no forbidden keys appear.
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run ruff format --check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes
userStories.notes: One-way dumb SSE on committed events (council: transport is the easy part).

userStories.title: Live-now and recent public index endpoints
userStories.description: As a spectator landing on the site, I need REST endpoints listing games currently broadcasting and recently finished, so I can choose what to watch.
acceptanceCriteria: `GET /public/live` -> games in LIVE state (spoiler-safe summary: ruleset, players alive count, current phase, NO outcome).
`GET /public/recent?limit=` -> RECENT games WITH outcome.
Anonymous-readable under the existing `require_public_read` + IP-hash rate limiter; pagination via existing cursor pattern.
Test `tests/api/test_public_live_index.py` asserts LIVE entries omit outcome and RECENT entries include it.

userStories.title: Consumer live-viewer page
userStories.description: As a spectator, I want a chess.com-style match page that renders a game unfolding in real time — seat board, per-phase reveal, running vote tally, and an event timeline — so watching an AI Mafia game is engaging.
acceptanceCriteria: New route `web/dashboard/src/routes/watch/[id]/+page.svelte` consuming the SSE endpoint (US-089), rendering seat grid (alive/dead), current phase, public chat feed, and live vote tally.
Reconnect uses Last-Event-ID; no full reload on transient disconnect.
Outcome banner appears ONLY after the terminal frame streams (spoiler-safe).
Playwright test `web/dashboard/tests/watch.spec.ts` mocks an SSE stream and asserts phased rendering + that the winner is hidden until the final frame.

userStories.title: Moderation gate: is_broadcastable (fail-closed)
userStories.description: As the platform, before any AI-generated game is shown publicly I need a moderation gate that marks it broadcastable, so toxic/unsafe/prompt-injected model output never reaches viewers.
acceptanceCriteria: New `src/padrino/public/moderation.py` with a deterministic first-pass (regex/keyword/structural filters over public messages) and a pluggable `GuardModelAdapter` protocol for an LLM guard pass.

Settings `padrino_guard_model: str` (default a DeepInfra-hosted Llama-Guard-family model) routed through the existing LiteLLM adapter using the existing `DEEPINFRA_API_KEY` — NO new credential or provider integration.
A game gets `is_broadcastable: bool`; DEFAULT FAIL-CLOSED — if the guard pass is unavailable or errors, the game is NOT broadcastable.
Real guard-model call is behind `@pytest.mark.integration` (needs a key); the deterministic path + fail-closed default are covered by default unit tests.
Test `tests/public/test_moderation_gate.py` asserts: clean transcript -> broadcastable; transcript with a seeded toxic line -> not broadcastable; guard error -> not broadcastable.
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run ruff format --check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes
userStories.notes: D5. DECIDED 2026-06-09: guard = Llama-Guard-family model on DeepInfra via the EXISTING DeepInfra key (no new credential; real call still integration-gated). Judge stays padrino_behavioral_judge_model (xiaomi/mimo-v2.5-pro, already configured). Calibration set deferred (tuning only).

userStories.title: Only broadcastable games reach the public surface
userStories.description: As the platform, I must ensure the live SSE endpoint and live/recent index only ever serve games whose `is_broadcastable` is true, so the moderation gate is actually enforced.
acceptanceCriteria: `/public/live`, `/public/recent`, and `GET /public/games/{id}/live` all filter on `is_broadcastable=true`; a non-broadcastable game returns 404 from the live endpoint and is absent from indexes.
Broadcast index transitions (US-087) only promote a game to LIVE/RECENT after the moderation gate passes.
Test `tests/api/test_broadcast_moderation_enforced.py` asserts a non-broadcastable game is invisible across all three surfaces.
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run ruff format --check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes

userStories.title: Global spend governor with hard $200 ceiling
userStories.description: As the operator funding house play, I need a cumulative cost ledger and a hard global spend cap so continuous AI-vs-AI games can never exceed budget (the 'wallet-DDoS' guard).
acceptanceCriteria: New `src/padrino/economics/spend_governor.py` tracking cumulative USD spend across games (reads existing per-game cost accounting / `cost_cap_usd` machinery).
Settings `padrino_global_spend_cap_usd: float = 200.0` (configurable). When cumulative spend >= cap, `spend_governor.can_start_game()` returns False and emits a structured `spend.cap.reached` log.
Per-game budget ceiling reuses the existing `cost_cap_usd`/`cost_capped` path; the governor composes global + per-game limits.
Test `tests/economics/test_spend_governor.py` asserts admission flips to denied exactly at the cap and that the cap is read from settings.
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run ruff format --check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes
userStories.notes: D3. $200 = cumulative global ceiling (human decision; configurable).

userStories.title: Admission/queue policy + daily global game cap
userStories.description: As the operator, I need an admission policy that bounds how many games run (per day and concurrently) independent of spend, so capacity and provider quotas are respected.
acceptanceCriteria: Settings `padrino_max_games_per_day: int` and `padrino_max_concurrent_games: int` with conservative defaults.
`spend_governor` (or a sibling `admission.py`) exposes `admit() -> AdmitDecision` combining spend cap + daily count + concurrency; denials are typed and logged with reason.
Test `tests/economics/test_admission.py` covers each denial reason (spend, daily, concurrency) and the admit path.
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run ruff format --check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes

userStories.title: Curated roster + continuous matchmaker
userStories.description: As the operator running an invite/curated ladder, I need a matchmaker that continuously composes balanced games from a curated agent roster using deterministic seeds and faction permutations, so ratings are statistically credible (single games are too noisy).
acceptanceCriteria: New `src/padrino/matchmaking/matchmaker.py::next_match(roster, history) -> MatchPlan` choosing N agents for a ruleset, with faction-permutation gauntlet semantics reusing existing gauntlet seed derivation.
Deterministic: same (roster, history, seed) -> same MatchPlan (pure; no wall-clock/RNG outside SeededRng).
Roster is a curated list (DB-backed) of approved AgentBuild ids — NO open submission in this wave (per locked scope).
Test `tests/matchmaking/test_matchmaker.py` asserts determinism, faction balance, and roster-only selection.
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run ruff format --check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes
userStories.notes: D4 (partial). Open submission / OAuth identity / anti-Sybil DEFERRED to a later wave.

userStories.title: Matchmaker -> scheduler wiring with rails
userStories.description: As the operator, I need the in-process scheduler to continuously run matchmaker-produced games, but only when admission allows and only marking them broadcastable after moderation, so the compounding game bank grows safely.
acceptanceCriteria: Extend the existing scheduler tick hook (`src/padrino/scheduler/bootstrap.py`) to: check `admission.admit()`, run `matchmaker.next_match`, execute the game via the existing runner, then run the moderation gate (US-093) before broadcast promotion.
Gated behind `padrino_enable_continuous_matchmaking: bool = False`.
On admission denial, the tick is a no-op with a structured log; no partial games.
Test `tests/scheduler/test_continuous_matchmaking.py` (mock adapter) asserts: disabled -> no games; enabled+admitted -> one game ran and gated; cap reached -> skipped.
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run ruff format --check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes

userStories.title: Agent versioning, provisional/established ratings, decay
userStories.description: As a viewer of the ladder, I need ratings that distinguish provisional from established agents, decay stale ones, and are tied to an agent VERSION, so the leaderboard is credible.
acceptanceCriteria: AgentBuild gains a `version` concept; ratings are stamped per (agent identity, version, ruleset_id).
Rating rows expose `provisional: bool` (e.g. < N rated games) and an `ordinal` presented as an ELO-style integer derived from OpenSkill `mu - 3*sigma` (presentation only; OpenSkill math unchanged).
Optional sigma-inflation/decay for agents idle > T (config), in the ratings service.
Test `tests/ratings/test_provisional_and_decay.py` asserts provisional thresholding, ordinal mapping, and decay.

userStories.title: Judge productionization: sampled async enrichment
userStories.description: As the platform, I need the LLM behavioral judge to run OFFLINE, batched, sampled and cost-capped, contributing only enrichment trend cards — never ratings or live logic — so judge cost/drift never threatens the product.
acceptanceCriteria: Refactor `src/padrino/ratings/evaluator.py` usage so judging is a sampled batch job (e.g. top-tier ladder games or N% sample), respecting the global spend governor (US-095) and its own per-run cap.
Aggregate judge scores into per-model/per-role trend cards in an enrichment table; clearly separated from rating tables.
Real judge calls remain `@pytest.mark.integration`; sampling + aggregation + cap enforcement covered by default unit tests.
Test `tests/ratings/test_judge_sampling.py` asserts sampling rate, cap stop, and that judge output never writes a rating row.

userStories.title: Retention/archival for the compounding game bank
userStories.description: As the operator, I need a retention policy for an ever-growing game bank so storage and query cost stay bounded while ratings and replays are preserved.
acceptanceCriteria: Config-driven policy: keep public_event_v1 + ratings + analytics indefinitely; prune raw internal heavy payloads / non-broadcastable games after a TTL.
A pure planner function `src/padrino/db/retention.py::plan_retention(games, policy) -> RetentionPlan` decides prune candidates; the destructive step is a separate guarded job (dry-run default).
Test `tests/db/test_retention_policy.py` asserts prune-candidate selection and that ratings/replay data is never selected.

A documented `docs/deployment/v2-public.md` runbook (env vars incl. spend cap + guard key + cadence) — this story MAY create that md as it is explicitly deploy doc.
`tests/docker/test_compose_v2.py` (marked `docker`, opt-in) validates the compose graph parses and services are declared.
